title: Ghit
description: Simple Minecraft mod that adds custom hitsounds to the game.
body:
# Ghit ( GalaxyHit )

Ghit ( GalaxyHit ) is a simple Minecraft mod that adds custom hitsounds to the game. You can set your own hitsounds, change their volume and pitch, or revert to the default Minecraft hitsound.

## Features

- **Set custom hitsound**: Choose your preferred sound for hits.
- **Clear hitsound**: Revert back to the default Minecraft hitsounds.
- **Adjust volume**: Control the volume of the hitsound.
- **Adjust pitch**: Control the pitch of the hitsound.

## In plans

- **Visual Hit Effect**: Make cool effects on entity hit

## Available Commands

### `/ghit set <name>`
Set a custom hitsound. Replace `<name>` with the name of suggested names for hitsounds.
Example: 
```bash
/ghit set Bling
```

### `/ghit clear`
Remove the custom hitsound and use the default Minecraft hitsound.
Example:
```bash
/ghit clear
```

### `/ghit volume <value>`
Change the volume of the hitsound. The value should be a number from `0.0` to `2.0` (default is `1.0`).
Example:
```bash
/ghit volume 0.5
```

### `/ghit pitch <value>`
Change the pitch of the hitsound. The value should be a number from `0.5` to `2.0` (default is `1.0`).
Example:
```bash
/ghit pitch 1.2
```

## Requirements:
- Minecraft 1.20.2 ( more versions soon.. )
- Fabric API

## Installation

1. Make sure you have [Fabric Loader](https://fabricmc.net/use/) installed.
2. Download and install the [Fabric API](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/fabric-api)
3. Place this mod in your Minecraft mods folder.

If you have any questions or issues, check out the [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/noxygalaxy/ghit-mod).

**Author**: @noxygalaxy
